items.title: Anthropic postmortem: Claude Code quality issues traced to three changes; prompt reverted in April 20 release
items.why_it_matters: Rare, concrete reliability write-up for a coding agent—useful lessons for evals, regressions, and rollout safety in agentic tooling.
items.brief: Anthropic details what broke Claude Code quality and what they reverted/fixed.
items.details: Anthropic published a postmortem on reports of degraded Claude Code quality. They attribute issues to three separate changes and note they reverted a prompt as part of an April 20 release. The write-up is valuable because it describes operational failure modes (quality regressions) rather than model capability claims. If you run coding agents in production, it’s a good checklist for monitoring and rollback discipline.
